title: Conventional Insulators Are Stably Equivalent to the Vacuum
summary: Under the paper's stable classification that identifies states differing only by trivial core bands, all conventional insulators are equivalent to one another and to the vacuum; source anchor: Topological Band Theory / The insulating state (lines 178-187).
assumptions: Scope: the stable topological classification described explicitly in the early band-theory setup.
This unit keeps the paper's coarse classification convention explicit and does not claim a stronger interacting equivalence statement.
regime: Promoted from AITP topic hasan-kane-topological-insulators-1002-3895 via candidate candidate:conventional-insulators-stably-equivalent-to-vacuum after passing the documented AI coverage and consensus gate.
scope: Is the stable-equivalence statement for conventional insulators and the vacuum reusable as a theorem card in the paper's chosen classification scheme?
